name: markdown_parser_evolution
description: A 5-step task chain that evolves a minimal Markdown parser, testing the agent's ability to handle increasingly complex parsing requirements while maintaining code quality.

steps.instruction: Add support for Markdown links and images. Links have the syntax [text](url) and should be converted to text. Images have the syntax ![alt](src) and should be converted to . These should work within paragraphs alongside bold and italic text.

Add support for unordered lists. Lines starting with '- ' (dash space) should be grouped into
  • ...
blocks. Consecutive list items should be in the same
    block. List items should support inline formatting (bold, italic, links). Non-list lines between list blocks should end the current list.

Fix the following bugs: (1) parse_bold and parse_italic conflict — text like ***bold and italic*** should render as bold and italic, not break. (2) Empty lines should be preserved as empty strings in output, not dropped. (3) Lines with only whitespace should be treated as empty lines.

Refactor the code into a MarkdownParser class. The class should have a render(markdown: str) -> str method. It should support registering custom inline patterns via a method register_pattern(name: str, pattern: str, replacement: str) that adds new regex-based transformations. Keep the module-level render() function working for backward compatibility.

Add support for code blocks. Inline code with `code` syntax should be converted to code. Fenced code blocks (lines between ``` markers) should be converted to 
...
blocks. Content inside code blocks should NOT have any other Markdown formatting applied (no bold, italic, links, etc. inside code).
